Fool is he who fails to notice arcane glyphs and wands and mistakes a clockwork mage for a common gear grinder or tinkerer.
The following class abilities are available to wizards of the clockwork school.

subclassFeature.name: Clockwork Savant
entries: Beginning when you choose this school at 2nd level, the gold and time it takes to copy a {@filter clockwork spell|spells|source=midgardheroes|components & miscellaneous=|subschool=clockwork} into your spell book is halved. You also gain proficiency with clockwork tools (25 gp, 2 lb).

subclassFeature.name: Clockworker's Charm
entries: Beginning at 2nd level, whenever you cast an {@spell animate construct|MidgardHeroes} spell, increase the duration by a number of minutes equal to your proficiency bonus. At 20th level, you can make the spell permanent until dispelled but cannot have more than one made permanent at a time.

subclassFeature.name: Clockwork Familiars
entries: Clockwork mages who use the spell find familiar substitute a small clockwork device in the form of an animal worth 10 gp for the spell's usual material components. It must resemble one of the allowable animals listed in the spell. When the ritual is complete, the clockwork animates. It has the statistics of the chosen form but is a construct. The familiar's form can be changed by rebuilding the clockwork device in the new form and casting find familiar again. If the familiar is destroyed, it can be rebuilt (in the same or a new form) with the same components, if they're recovered; otherwise, 10 gp must be spent on replacement parts. The familiar is never counted when determining the number of constructs a mage can control.

subclassFeature.name: Metal Shape
entries: When you reach 6th level, you gain the ability to reshape metal with a touch. When you grasp a piece of metal of Small size or smaller, you can alter its form into any shape that suits your purpose. The item must be in your hands and under your control; you can't, for example, reshape a piece of armor or a weapon that's being worn or wielded by someone else. To create a specific object, such as a key or mechanical component, you must be completely familiar with it. Thus you could replicate a key that you had in your possession for an extended period of time, but you could not create a working key based on seeing the lock alone.

Objects you create can have up to two hinges and a latch, but finer mechanical detail is not possible. This effect can be used to repair metal as per the spell. You may use this feature once before completing a long or short rest.

subclassFeature.name: Golem Form
entries: Beginning at 10th level, you can transform yourself as an action into a living construct for up to 1 minute per level. You retain your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma and the ability to speak and cast spells. You can transform into a golem or a clockwork creature whose CR is less than or equal to your current level in this class. Otherwise, this ability functions as the druid's {@classFeature Wild Shape|Druid||2} ability.

subclassFeature.name: Clockwork Mastery
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can use magic to bring constructs under your control. As an action, you can compel one {@filter construct|bestiary|source=|type=construct|miscellaneous=} you can see within 60 feet of you to make an Intelligence saving throw against your wizard spell save DC. This is a magical effect. If the saving throw fails, the construct becomes friendly to you and obeys your commands for 1 hour, until you use this ability again, or until it takes damage from you or one of your allies. If the saving throw fails by 5 or more, the duration is extended to 6 hours or until one of the other conditions is fulfilled. When the effect ends, the construct is aware it was controlled by you.

subclassFeature.name: Doom Croaker
entries: The doom croaker tradition originated among the Northlands ravenfolk and spread from there to the northern dwarves. Doom croakers follow the teachings of Wotan to gain glimpses into the infinite knowledge he was granted while hanging from the branches of Yggdrasil.

Common folk regard doom croakers with a mixture of fear and awe. The doom croakers carry the power of Wotan's runes, and though people cautiously seek out croakers for their wisdom, they remain terrified of giving offense and rousing a doom croaker's ire.

The rune magic of doom croakers is substantially different from the runes crafted by runecasters (see chapter 6). Except as noted below, doom croakers function as wizards and use the wizard spell list.

subclassFeature.name: Divination Savant
entries: Beginning when you select this tradition at 2nd level, the gold and time you must spend to copy a {@filter divination spell|spells|source=|components & miscellaneous=|school=d} into your spellbook is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Revelation of Runes
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can cast {@spell alarm} as a ritual by tracing a rune onto the floor, ground, or other surface.

Additionally, at 5th level you add {@spell glyph of warding} to your spellbook, if it isn't there already. You can cast {@spell glyph of warding} without expending a spell slot and without requiring material components. You can only have one glyph of warding cast in this way at one time. If you use this ability to cast another, the first glyph ends immediately.
Once you cast {@spell glyph of warding} this way, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, though you can still cast it normally using an available spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Raven's Eye
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you add {@spell clairvoyance} to your spellbook, if it isn't there already. You can cast {@spell clairvoyance} without expending a spell slot. When you do, the casting time of the spell is 1 action.
Once you cast {@spell clairvoyance} this way, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, though you can still cast it normally using an available spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Wotan's Eye
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can catch glimpses of the future. As a reaction after you or a creature targeted by your spells makes a saving throw, you can magically force the creature to reroll. The creature must accept the second result.
You can use this ability once, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Branches of Yggdrasil
entries: At 14th level, you add {@spell legend lore} to your spellbook, if it isn't there already. When you are at 0 hit points, you can reroll your first failed death saving throw.

If you die, your soul hangs in the branches of the world tree for seven days, after which time a huginn egg appears in the place where you died. You hatch from the egg in a new body as if a {@spell reincarnate spell} was cast on you. Once you use this ability, you can't use it again for 1 month.

subclassFeature.name: Dragon Masks
entries: Wizards practice the subtle magic of pulling power from thin air and shaping it to fit their needs. Sorcerers tame the chaotic power within themselves, releasing it in barely controlled gouts of magical eruptions. Mages who walk the path between these two diverse means of magic are called dragon magi, and they meld the order of dweomery with the chaos of sorcery to grant themselves powers beyond anything the individual traditions could muster alone.

Dragon magi manifest their magic in variety of styles, as diverse as the draconic species and the types of magic that populate the worlds. Kobolds serving as councilors to great wyrms are taught by their masters to harness their innate draconic natures through the use of more traditional spells. A wild-eyed human, oozing sorcerous magic from an ancient draconic ancestor, might find a kindly wizard to help her focus her power. Special legions of dragonborn troops train with a master dragon mage, learning all there is to know to better defend their homeland from invaders.
Except as noted below, dragon magi function as wizards and use the wizard spell list.

entries.name: The Mechanics of the Dragon Mage: The Dragon Aspect
entries: The dragon mage arcane tradition takes a new look at the traditional wizard class, using the spell slot system as a basis for powering different magical abilities. In some ways, this arcane tradition sacrifices some of the flexibility of the wizard class for a more clearly defined set of powers and abilities, useful in both close-quarters combat and at range.

As they increase in level, wizards who focus on dragon magic learn to invoke what are known as dragon aspects. At lower levels, the dragon mage is only able to create one dragon aspect: the dragon mask. As they increase in power and experience, they can manifest up to four different dragon aspects. These aspects manifest visually as a translucent draconic feature. For example, the dragon mask appears as a colorful dragon's head. Observers see the mask as a wavering field of energy, but they can also see the dragon mage's face beneath it.
Similarly, the dragon wings aspect manifests as a semisolid pair of strong wings sprouting from the dragon mage's back.

Each dragon aspect can be manifested by using a spell slot. Once manifested, the dragon aspect provides a set of benefits to the dragon mage. In addition, spell slots can be burned to provide additional benefits via the dragon aspect. Dragon aspects are magical enhancements to the dragon mage that can be dispelled, but no {@status concentration} is required to maintain an aspect. A dragon aspect can be dispelled with a bonus action, and a dragon mage who can manifest more than one dragon aspect can switch from one aspect to another as a bonus action; however, a spell slot must be spent to power the new aspect, even if there is time remaining on the old one.

subclassFeature.name: Dragon Magic Spells
entries: The following spells are added to the spell lists of dragon magi.

entries.caption: Angelic Spell List
rows: {@spell converse with dragon|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell draconic smite|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell kobold's fury|MidgardHeroes}
{@spell detect dragons|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell enhance greed|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell lair sense|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell shade|MidgardHeroes}
{@spell claws of the earth dragon|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell dragon breath|MidgardHeroes}

subclassFeature.name: Invoke Dragon Mask
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can invoke a magical dragon mask. You use a bonus action and spend a spell slot to invoke the mask, which lasts for 3 rounds per level of the spent spell slot. The dragon mask remains as long as you are not {@condition incapacitated}, until the duration ends, or until it is dismissed or replaced with a bonus action. It is a translucent magical force, in the form of a dragon's head, which covers your face. Your face is still visible beneath the mask, and the mask does not hinder vision.
While you wear your dragon mask, you gain the following benefits and drawbacks.

items: You gain a bonus to your AC equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1).

You gain a bite attack. As an action, make a melee spell attack against one adjacent target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} + Int modifier piercing damage. This attack counts as magical for purposes of overcoming a target's immunity or resistance to piecing damage.
You gain advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks, as well as on Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks.
Any ranged or melee attack spells you cast while wearing the dragon mask are made with disadvantage, and saving throws against spells you cast while wearing the dragon mask are made with advantage.

As a bonus action, you can spend an additional spell slot to augment your dragon mask. Your next dragon mask's bite attack damage increases by{@dice  + 1d8} per level of the spell slot spent, and you gain advantage on the attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Invoke Dragon Heart
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can invoke a magical dragon heart. You use a bonus action and spend a spell slot to invoke the dragon heart, which lasts for 3 rounds per level of the spent spell slot. The dragon heart remains as long as you are not {@condition incapacitated}, until the duration ends, or until it is dismissed or replaced with a bonus action. The dragon heart is a translucent magical force, in the form of a beating heart, which covers your chest.
While you wear your dragon heart, you gain the following benefits.

items: You gain a bonus to Wisdom and Charisma saving throws equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1).
You gain temporary hit points equal to twice your wizard level. These temporary hit points are lost when your dragon heart aspect is removed or lost for any reason.

You gain a breath weapon attack. As an action, a 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide line of energy (acid, cold, fire, or lightning) shoots from your dragon heart in a direction you choose. Each creature in the line must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes {@damage 4d6} damage on a failed save, and no damage on a successful one. The type of damage can be selected when making the attack and cannot be changed until a new dragon heart is manifested.

As a bonus action, you can spend an additional spell slot to augment your dragon heart. Your next dragon heart's breath weapon attack damage increases by{@dice  + 2d6} per level of the spell slot spent, and you can add 10 feet of length to the line per level of the spell slot spent.

subclassFeature.name: Invoke Dragon Wings
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can invoke a set of magical dragon wings. You use a bonus action and spend a spell slot to invoke the dragon wings, which last for 2 rounds per level of the spent spell slot. The dragon wings remain as long as you are not {@condition incapacitated}, until the duration ends, or until they are dismissed or replaced with a bonus action.
While you wear your dragon wings, you gain the following benefits.

items: Your speed increases by 10 feet, and you gain a fly speed equal to your walking speed.
You gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical sources.
You gain advantage on any melee or ranged spell attack rolls.

As a bonus action, you can spend an additional spell slot to augment your dragon wings. Until the start of your next turn, you can add 5 feet of additional speed per level of the spell slot spent. Additionally, you can choose one creature per level of the spell slot spent within 10 feet of you, and ranged attacks against those creatures are made at disadvantage.

subclassFeature.name: Invoke Dragon Tail
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can invoke a magical dragon tail. You use a bonus action and spend a spell slot to invoke the dragon tail, which last for 1 round per level of the spent spell slot. The dragon tail remains as long as you are not {@condition incapacitated}, until the duration ends, or until it is dismissed or replaced with a bonus action. The dragon tail is a translucent magical force, in the form of a 15-foot-long appendage capable of striking with incredible force.
While you wear your dragon tail, you gain the following benefits.

items: You are immune to the {@condition grappled} condition.

You are proficient with Strength and Dexterity saving throws, and any skill checks using Strength. In addition, you can use your Intelligence modifier instead of the regular modifier for those saving throws and skill checks.

You gain a tail slam attack. As an action, make a separate melee spell attack against all targets within 15 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 3d10} + Int modifier bludgeoning damage. Targets hit by this attack are pushed up to 10 feet away from you and knocked {@condition prone} unless they succeed on a Strength saving throw against your spell DC. This attack counts as magical for purposes of overcoming a target's immunity or resistance to bludgeoning damage.
As a reaction, you can make a tail slam attack against a creature who enters your tail's reach.

As a bonus action, you can spend an additional spell slot to augment your dragon tail. Until the start of your next turn, your dragon tail's slam attack damage increases by{@dice  + 1d10} per level of the spell slot spent. In addition, you heal yourself for 3 hit points per level of the spell slot spent.

subclassFeature.name: Elementalist
entries: You focus your arcane studies on the power inherent to one of the Elemental Planes. Elementalists believe in the superiority of their chosen element above all others, and they aren't shy about making that fact known. Elementalists are sometimes secluded researchers, but more often they're adventurous types who sign on with groups that can appreciate their singular talents.
Except as noted below, elementalists function as wizards and use the wizard spell list.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Savant
entries: Beginning when you select this tradition at 2nd level, choose one element from the table below. That element becomes the focus of your study. You can speak, read, and write the language associated with your focus, and the associated damage type is used by other features as noted.

Some spells resonate with your focus element. They might deal the associated damage type, manipulate the element in question, or otherwise embody an elemental theme. Work with your GM to determine whether a given spell resonates with your focus. The gold and time you must spend to copy a spell resonant with your chosen element into your spellbook is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Mastery
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you learn techniques called masteries to channel elemental magic into your being and spells.

entries.name: Masteries
entries: You learn two masteries of your choice, which are detailed under "Masteries" below. You learn one additional mastery of your choice at 6th, 10th, and 14th levels. Each time you learn a new mastery, you can also replace one mastery you know with a different one.

subclassFeature.name: Masteries
entries: Masteries are specialized techniques you can use to augment your spellcasting. Unless noted otherwise, you can use only one mastery on a given spell. The masteries are presented in alphabetical order.

subclassFeature.name: Substitute Elements
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you can funnel elemental essence through your damage-dealing spells. When you cast a {@filter spell that does damage|spells|source=|components & miscellaneous=|subschool=clockwork|damage type=acid;bludgeoning;cold;fire;force;lightning;necrotic;piercing;poison;psychic;radiant;slashing;thunder}, you can change the damage to the type associated with your {@subclassFeature Elemental Savant|Wizard||Elementalist|MidgardHeroes|2||elemental focus}.

subclassFeature.name: Absorb ELements
entries: Starting at 10th level, when you would take damage of the type associated with your {@subclassFeature Elemental Savant|Wizard||Elementalist|MidgardHeroes|2||elemental focus}, you can use a reaction to take no damage and regain a number of hit points equal to half the damage you would have taken. You can use this ability even if you have resistance or immunity to that damage type from another source. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Irresistible Strike
entries: At 14th level, your elemental damage spells are unstoppable. Your spells that deal damage of the type associated with your {@subclassFeature Elemental Savant|Wizard||Elementalist|MidgardHeroes|2||elemental focus} ignore immunity to that damage type. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Elven High Magic
entries: Elven high magic is very powerful and extremely rare. With high magic and sufficient time, an elven wizard can accomplish just about anything. It is a closely guarded secret, practiced only by the elves and their shadow fey relatives. Non-elves are never taught the ways of this school. That's both a sign of the elves' sense of superiority and an unfortunate practicality. High magic can take centuries to master, and humans and other short-lived races simply don't live long enough to become skilled in this art.
Except as noted below, practitioners of elven high magicfunction as wizards and use the wizard spell list.

entries.subclassFeature: Elven High Magic Spells|Wizard||Elven High Magic|MidgardHeroes|2

subclassFeature.name: Ritual Savant
entries: Beginning at 2nd level when you select this school, the gold and time you must spend to copy a {@filter high magic spell|spells|source=|components & miscellaneous=|subschool=high elven} into your spellbook is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Ritual Focus
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can spend 10 minutes centering your mental energy and creating a ritual focus. When you choose to do so, you can expend your ritual focus to create one of the following effects when casting the ritual version of a spell.

items: You can cast a ritual version of a spell without adding the additional 10 minutes to the casting time that's normally required.

You can expend a spell slot equal to or higher than the spell's nominal level as you cast the ritual version of the spell, provided the spell allows that as an option. The ritual takes effect at a level one higher than the expended spell slot instead of the minimum. You must be able to cast spells of the ritual's elevated level for the ritual to take effect.

entries: Some spells have additional effects that you can invoke by expending your ritual focus. You can't use your ritual focus again until after you complete a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Elven High Magic Spells
entries: These spells are added to the spell lists of practitioners of elven high magic.

entries.caption: Elven High Magic Spell List
rows: {@spell black swan storm|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell clearing the field|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell heartache|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell shadows brought to light|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell vine trestle|MidgardHeroes}
{@spell bloom|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell desolation|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell harsh light of summer's glare|MidgardHeroes}

subclassFeature.name: Bound Magic
entries: At 6th level, you begin to master an understanding of elven high magic. You bind magic into the fabric of a place, making it last without any concentration. Choose a ritual spell you know that targets an area. Cast the ritual version of the spell and expend your ritual focus while burning expensive herbs worth 200 gp per level of the ritual. The spell's duration in that location becomes a year and a day. If you cast the ritual every day for 30 consecutive days, the spell becomes permanent in that location until it's dispelled or otherwise destroyed.
Additionally, you can expend your ritual focus twice between rests.

subclassFeature.name: Ritual Master
entries: Beginning at 10th level, you can copy {@filter any spells with the ritual tag|spells|source=|components & miscellaneous=ritual}, regardless of class, into your spellbook. Ritual spells that you copy into your spellbook using this ability must be no higher in level than half of your wizard level (rounded up). You can cast these spells only as rituals unless you also learn them by other means.

subclassFeature.name: Radiant Countenance
entries: Starting at 14th level, ritual versions of spells that you cast are treated as if they were cast using a spell slot one level higher than the slot you actually use. High magic can be combined with the increased spell level benefit of expending your ritual focus. This applies only to spells that can be cast at higher levels.
Additionally, you can expend your ritual focus three times between rests

subclassFeature.name: Entropy
entries: Entropy is very powerful and extremely rare. With high magic and sufficient time, an elven wizard can accomplish just about anything. It is a closely guarded secret, practiced only by the elves and their shadow fey relatives. Non-elves are never taught the ways of this school. That's both a sign of the elves' sense of superiority and an unfortunate practicality. High magic can take centuries to master, and humans and other short-lived races simply don't live long enough to become skilled in this art.
Except as noted below, practitioners of Entropyfunction as wizards and use the wizard spell list.

subclassFeature.name: Chaos Savant
entries: Beginning at 2nd level when you select this school, the gold and time you must spend to copy a {@filter chaos spell|spells|source=midgardheroes|components & miscellaneous=|subschool=chaos} into your spellbook is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Long Odds
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can manipulate the reality-changing forces of chaos to alter the odds of a situation. You can give yourself (as a bonus action) or a creature you can see (as a reaction) advantage on one attack roll, saving throw, or ability check that will be made this turn. Doing this causes a {@subclassFeature Chaos Magic Surges|Wizard||Entropy|MidgardHeroes|2||chaos magic surge}. You must finish a long rest before you can use this ability again.

subclassFeature.name: Chaos Magic Surges
entries: Using chaos magic sometimes causes a chaos magic surge. These are similar to wild magic surges caused by sorcerers. When a chaos magic surge occurs, you must roll {@dice 1d20}. If you roll a 1, consult the {@table Chaos Magic Surge|MidgardHeroes} table to determine the specific result.

subclassFeature.name: Twisted Arcana
entries: Starting at 6th level, when you cast a spell that causes damage, you can infuse the spell with greater power by channeling chaos through yourself. You make this choice after determining whether the spell has been successful (i.e., after making a spell attack roll) but before determining damage. If you invoke twisted arcana, roll {@dice 1d6 + 1}; this is the maximum number of your spell's damage dice you can reroll. You can reroll any number of damage dice up to that maximum, and you must use the rerolled results.
In addition, the type of damage your spell causes is replaced randomly. Use the {@table Damage Type|MidgardHeroes} table to determine the spell's new damage type.
Using this ability causes a {@subclassFeature Chaos Magic Surges|Wizard||Entropy|MidgardHeroes|2||chaos magic surge}. You must finish a long rest before you can use this ability again.

subclassFeature.name: Shifting Resistance
entries: Beginning at 10th level, when you cast a {@filter chaos magic spell|spells|source=midgardheroes|components & miscellaneous=|subschool=chaos}, you gain resistance to a random damage type for a number of rounds equal to 1 + your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1). You can choose to cause a {@subclassFeature Chaos Magic Surges|Wizard||Entropy|MidgardHeroes|2||chaos magic surge} as part of your casting; if you do, roll twice on the {@table Damage Type|MidgardHeroes} table and choose the type you prefer, then determine the effect (if any) of the {@subclassFeature Chaos Magic Surges|Wizard||Entropy|MidgardHeroes|2||chaos magic surge}. You must finish a long rest before you can use this ability again.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Chaos
entries: Starting at 14th level, anytime you cause a {@subclassFeature Chaos Magic Surges|Wizard||Entropy|MidgardHeroes|2||chaos magic surge}, you regain one use of {@subclassFeature Long Odds|Wizard||Entropy|MidgardHeroes|2}, {@subclassFeature Twisted Arcana|Wizard||Entropy|MidgardHeroes|6}, or {@subclassFeature Shifting Resistance|Wizard||Entropy|MidgardHeroes|10} without taking a long rest. In addition, every time you cast a {@filter chaos spell|spells|source=midgardheroes|components & miscellaneous=|subschool=chaos}, you gain temporary hit points equal to your Intelligence modifier + the spell's level.

subclassFeature.name: Geomancy
entries: While any spellcaster can tap the potential coursing through ley lines, the tradition of geomancy teaches the art of becoming like a ley line and bonding with the great flow of power. Geomancy is sometimes looked down on by adherents of traditions that focus on individual magic schools because it eschews specialization in favor of molding any form of magic through the focused power of ley lines.

Geomancers have an increased facility for tapping into any ley line, but they also learn to bind themselves to a specific ley line. While other spellcasters must be within a ley line's area of influence to use it, the geomancer becomes a tributary of his or her chosen river of power, capable of siphoning its power anywhere.
Except as noted below, geomancers function as wizards and use the wizard spell list.

subclassFeature.name: Ley Line Savant
entries: Beginning when you select this tradition at 2nd level, the gold and time you must spend to copy a {@filter ley line spell|spells|source=midgardheroes|components & miscellaneous=|subschool=ley line} into your spellbook is halved.
When you gain a level, one of the two spells you learn for gaining a level can be a ley line magic spell, even if you've never encountered it before.

Additionally, you can sense the presence of nearby ley lines. As an action, you can discern the distance to the closest ley line within 1 mile, as well as the direction to every ley line within 1 mile.

subclassFeature.name: Ley Magic Spell List
entries: The following spells are normally available to geomancers (in addition to the wizard spell list), but they can also be used by druids, sorcerers, and warlocks who have a ley line feat ({@feat ley initiate|MidgardHeroes} or {@feat ley-bound|MidgardHeroes}). Ley magic spells can be learned only by discovering written versions of them, unless the GM allows otherwise.

entries.caption: Elven High Magic Spell List
rows: {@spell amplify ley field|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell energy absorption|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell lesser ley pulse|MidgardHeroes}, {@spell ley leech|MidgardHeroes}

subclassFeature.name: Channel Ley Line
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you learn to tap the power of ley lines to augment your spellcasting, as described in {@variantrule Ley Lines and Geomancy|MidgardHeroes|chapter 6}.

Additionally, when you finish a short or long rest within 1 mile of a weak ley line, you can bind yourself to that ley line. You can be bound to only one ley line at a time. If you later bind yourself to a second ley line, you lose your connection to the previous one.

While you are bound to a ley line, you can channel the power of the line into your spellcasting from any distance, as long as you are on the same plane as the ley line. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Ley Line Manipulation
entries: Beginning at 6th level, when you augment your magic with power from a {@variantrule Ley Lines and Geomancy|MidgardHeroes|ley line}, you can reroll the effect of an unlocked ley line or the result of a backlash. You must use the rerolled result. After rerolling, you can't use a reaction until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Ley Line Adept
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can bind yourself to weak or strong ley lines with your {@subclassFeature Channel Ley Line|Wizard||Geomancy|MidgardHeroes|2} feature. In addition, you add your proficiency bonus to the ability check when tapping a ley line for power.

subclassFeature.name: Ley Line Mastery
entries: At 14th level, you can bind yourself to {@table ley line effects, weak|MidgardHeroes|weak}, {@table ley line effects, strong|MidgardHeroes|strong}, or {@table ley line effects, titanic|MidgardHeroes|titanic ley lines} with your {@subclassFeature Channel Ley Line|Wizard||Geomancy|MidgardHeroes|2} feature, and you have advantage on the ability check to tap the ley line.

As an action, you can lock or unlock a ley line within 30 feet of you. When you lock a ley line, choose one of its effects; the ley line will always have that effect for you while it remains locked. Once you use this feature to lock or unlock a ley line, you can't do so again until you finish a number of long rests depending on the intensity of the line: one for a weak ley line, two for strong, and three for titanic.

subclassFeature.name: Illumination
entries: First created by the shadow fey, the school of illumination blends observations of the heavens with the manipulation of light and shadow into an arcane tradition that's closely tied with the shadow plane. Many of its spells have much in common with divination, illusion, and necromancy. Unlike those schools, illumination magic is obscure and rarely practiced outside the shadow plane. Its practitioners have been likened to elementalists who manipulate shadow instead of fire, earth, air, or water. This school is not simply about controlling shadow, however; its focus is simultaneously light and the absence of light. Starlight, in particular, holds special meaning within this school. Illumination mages study the evershifting alignment of the stars in their effort to read the road map of fate—a study that inevitably leads them out of the starless shadow plane and into contact with the mortal world.

Members of the School of Illumination, called illuminators, use the stars to predict when danger is near, and draw on the power of darkness to attack their foes. This school is popular with delvers into the underworld, because its spells work best where light is dim or absent.
Except as noted below, illuminators function as wizards and use the wizard spell list.

subclassFeature.name: Illumination Savant
entries: Beginning when you select this school at 2nd level, the gold and time needed to copy {@filter illumination spells|spells|source=midgardheroes|components & miscellaneous=|subschool=illumination} into your spellbook is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Omen of Warning
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can forecast danger for the next 24 hours by studying the stars for one hour. The stars must be visible to you for you to use this ability. Studying the stars this way gives you advantage on up to two initiative checks. The bonus remains available for 24 hours or until the end of your next long rest. Instead of using an omen yourself, you can grant advantage on an initiative check to one other creature you can see when initiative checks are being made, but this prevents you from using the benefit yourself in that combat. You can choose whether to use an omen at the moment when initiative is rolled, but you must make the decision before rolling the die.

subclassFeature.name: Master of the Endless Night
entries: At 6th level, the spell attack modifier and spell save DC for {@filter cantrips|spells|source=|level=0|components & miscellaneous=} you cast in {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light or darkness} increases by 1.

subclassFeature.name: Illusions of Permanence
entries: Beginning at 10th level, {@filter illusions|spells|source=|components & miscellaneous=concentration|school=i} cast by you that require {@status concentration} last for 1 round after you lose {@status concentration} or stop {@status concentration||concentrating}, provided the spell hasn't exceeded its maximum duration.

subclassFeature.name: Comprehension of the Starry Sky
entries: Starting at 14th level, during a long rest you can consult the stars and comprehend some meaning in a cosmic event. The stars must be visible to you for you to use this ability. The insight you gather is stored as a small reserve of magic inside an item that has meaning to you; a star chart or astrolabe is commonly used, but any item that can be held in your hand will suffice. When the item is in your hand, you can consume that stored magic as a bonus action to invoke one of the following effects. The insight is expended by one use, and it's lost if it hasn't been used within 24 hours or by the start of your next long rest.

entries.name: Comet:
entries: Comets are the harbingers of change and instability. You can change your appearance as if you'd cast an {@spell alter self} spell, but the effect doesn't require {@status concentration} and lasts until you take a long rest.

entries.name: Conjunction:
entries: Planetary conjunctions destabilize minds and emotions. You can give one creature you can see disadvantage on a saving throw against one enchantment or illusion spell cast by you.

entries.name: Eclipse:
entries: Eclipses plunge the world into darkness and strengthen connections to the shadow plane. When you cast a spell of 5th level or lower that causes necrotic damage, you can reroll a number of damage dice up to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of one). You must use the new rolls.

entries.name: Nova:
entries: The nova is a powerful aid to divination spells. You can treat one divination spell you cast as though you had used a spell slot one level higher than the slot actually used.

subclassFeature.name: Necrophagy
entries: The necrophage tradition is similar to that of the necromancer, but with a much more grisly focus. Necrophages, sometimes called "death eaters," consume the flesh of both the living and the recently deceased to steal those creatures' knowledge and power.

Adherents of this tradition claim that it is the origin of all necromancy magic and necrotic energy. They view the Negative Plane as a cosmic necrophage all its own, devouring all matter, energy, and life and replacing it with necrotic energy and undead.
Except as noted below, necrophages function as wizards and use the wizard spell list.

subclassFeature.name: Necromancy Savant
entries: Beginning when you select this tradition at 2nd level, the gold and time you must spend to copy a {@filter necromancy spell|spells|source=|components & miscellaneous=|school=n} into your spellbook is halved

subclassFeature.name: Undead Familiar
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you add the {@spell find familiar} spell to your spellbook if you don't already have it. When you conjure a familiar, the creature is undead instead of celestial, fey, or fiend. The familiar gains the trait Undead Fortitude: If damage reduces the familiar to 0 hit points, it makes a Constitution saving throw with a DC = 5 + the damage taken, unless the damage is radiant or from a critical hit. On a success, the familiar drops to 1 hit point instead.

subclassFeature.name: Memory of Flesh
entries: Beginning at 6th level, as an action you can consume the flesh of a living, undead, or recently slain (within 1 hour) creature with Intelligence 6 or greater. You gain temporary hit points equal to your wizard level, and if the creature was proficient in any Intelligence skills, you can pick one of them. You gain proficiency in that skill or, if you are already proficient, you can add double your normal proficiency bonus when using that skill. You can't use this ability on the same creature again within 24 hours. If you are still under the effect of this ability when you use it on a different creature, the original effect ends. If your Undead Familiar is within 100 feet when you use this ability, it also gains the benefit.
The temporary hit points and absorbed skill proficiency last until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Fruit of the Mind
entries: Starting at 10th level, when you use {@subclassFeature Memory of Flesh|Wizard||Necrophagy|MidgardHeroes|6}, you can strip information from the creature as if you cast {@spell speak with dead}, even if the creature is alive or undead. The creature has disadvantage on its saving throw. If the saving throw is successful, you can't use this ability on the same creature again for 24 hours. You can use this ability on a single creature once for each point of its Constitution modifier (minimum of 1). Otherwise, its usage limits are the same as those for {@subclassFeature Memory of Flesh|Wizard||Necrophagy|MidgardHeroes|6}.

subclassFeature.name: Feed on Life
entries: At 14th level, after using {@subclassFeature Memory of Flesh|Wizard||Necrophagy|MidgardHeroes|6} or {@subclassFeature Fruit of the Mind|Wizard||Necrophagy|MidgardHeroes|10}, you no longer require air, food, drink, or sleep; you gain immunity to disease, to being {@condition poisoned}, and to poison and necrotic damage; and your hit point maximum can't be reduced. These effects last for 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Ring Warden
entries: The necrophage tradition is similar to that of the necromancer, but with a much more grisly focus. Necrophages, sometimes called "death eaters," consume the flesh of both the living and the recently deceased to steal those creatures' knowledge and power.

Adherents of this tradition claim that it is the origin of all necromancy magic and necrotic energy. They view the Negative Plane as a cosmic necrophage all its own, devouring all matter, energy, and life and replacing it with necrotic energy and undead.
Except as noted below, necrophages function as wizards and use the wizard spell list.

subclassFeature.name: Transmutation Savant
entries: Beginning when you select this tradition at 2nd level, the gold and time you must spend to copy a {@filter transmutation spell|spells|source=|components & miscellaneous=|school=t} into your spellbook is halved

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: At 2nd level, you gain proficiency in your choice of {@item blacksmith's tools|PHB|blacksmith's} or {@item jeweler's tools|PHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Bonded Ring-Staff
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you create a special {@item quarterstaff|PHB} with two metal rings embedded in it or fastened around it. You can use your ring-staff as an arcane focus. Creating an additional ring takes 8 hours of work with {@item blacksmith's tools|PHB|blacksmith's} or {@item jeweler's tools|PHB} and costs 5 gp in raw materials. Your ring-staff can incorporate a number of rings equal to your wizard level.

When you roll damage for a spell while holding your ring-staff, you can add your proficiency bonus to the result. If the spell generates multiple damage rolls against multiple targets (such as scorching ray), you must choose which roll gains the benefit before you roll it. You can use this ability a number of times equal to the number of metal rings fashioned into your ring-staff, up to a maximum of your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1). You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Master Metalsmith
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you can add double your normal proficiency bonus with the tools you chose at 2nd level. When using your chosen tools to craft mundane or magical items, you make double the normal progress each day. Additionally, you learn the formula to craft a single type of magical ring chosen from the following list: {@item ring of jumping}, {@item ring of mind shielding}, {@item ring of protection}, {@item ring of the ram}, {@item ring of regeneration}, {@item ring of resistance}. At the GM's discretion, you can choose a ring not listed here. You must meet all the standard prerequisites for crafting the ring and pay the required gold as normal, along with any special materials or conditions set by the GM.

subclassFeature.name: Imbue Ring
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can imbue magic into the rings embedded in your ring-staff. When you cast a spell, you can choose to imbue the spell into one of the rings. As long as the spell is imbued, you can't regain the expended spell slot. As an action, you can remove an imbued ring from the {@item staff|PHB} and give it to another creature (or hold it yourself). A creature holding an imbued ring can release the imbued spell as an action. {@action Attack|XPHB} rolls, saving throws, and damage are based on the caster who imbued the ring, but the creature holding the ring counts as the spell's caster for all other purposes.
An imbued ring is considered an ongoing spell for purposes of {@spell dispel magic}. You can have a number of rings imbued at one time equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Ring Bond
entries: At 14th level, with 1 hour of work (which can be done during a short rest), you can embed a single magical ring into your ring-staff. As long as you hold the ring-staff, you gain the effects of the embedded ring as if you were attuned to it and wearing it (including any negative effects of attuning to the item, such as curses). This ring doesn't count as one of your three attuned magic items.
You can remove an embedded ring (along with its attunement) with 1 hour of work, and you can replace it with a new one at the same time.

optionalfeature.name: Clinch
entries: Immediately after you hit with a {@filter finesse weapon|items|source=|type=|property=finesse|category=|miscellaneous=}, you can spend 2 {@subclassFeature Prowess|Rogue||Duelist|MidgardHeroes|3} to automatically {@action grapple} the creature you hit. Normal size and reach restrictions apply. Select one of the creature's attack actions; for as long as the grapple persists, the creature has disadvantage with that attack. You can maintain this grapple even if you hold a {@item dagger|PHB} in your offhand.

optionalfeature.name: Deep Slice
entries: When you inflict damage with a {@filter finesse weapon|items|source=|type=|property=finesse|category=|miscellaneous=}, you can spend 1 {@subclassFeature Prowess|Rogue||Duelist|MidgardHeroes|3} to deal a deep, bleeding wound. At the start of its turn, the bleeding creature must make a successful Constitution saving throw or take 3 ({@damage 1d6}) piercing damage. On a successful save, the effect ends. The creature or its adjacent ally can end the effect by spending an action to use a {@item healer's kit|PHB} or to make a successful Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) check against your technique save DC. Any amount of healing also ends the effect. A deep slice has no effect against constructs and undead.

optionalfeature.name: Disarming Parry
entries: When you are missed by a melee attack, you can spend 1 {@subclassFeature Prowess|Rogue||Duelist|MidgardHeroes|3} as a reaction to force the attacker to make a Strength saving throw. If it fails, the attacker drops the weapon it attacked with. If you have a free hand, you can catch the weapon with a successful DC 10 Dexterity check; otherwise it lands 10 feet away from the attacker in a random direction.

optionalfeature.name: Effortless Steps
entries: When you take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action, you can spend 1 {@subclassFeature Prowess|Rogue||Duelist|MidgardHeroes|3} to ignore nonmagical {@quickref difficult terrain||3}, and climbing doesn't cost extra movement until the end of your turn. You can spend 1 additional prowess to move through a single foe's space without triggering an {@action opportunity attack} from that creature.

optionalfeature.name: Pommel Punch
entries: When you make an attack, you can spend 2 {@subclassFeature Prowess|Rogue||Duelist|MidgardHeroes|3} to strike the target with the pommel of your weapon instead of the blade. This does {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage instead of the weapon's normal damage dice, and the target must make a successful Constitution saving throw or be {@condition incapacitated} until the end of its next turn. The target can be no more than one size category larger than you.

optionalfeature.name: Rapid Strikes
entries: If you make a second attack with a {@filter light weapon|items|source=|type=|property=light|category=|miscellaneous=} in your off-hand, you can spend 1 {@subclassFeature Prowess|Rogue||Duelist|MidgardHeroes|3} to add your ability bonus to this attack's damage roll.

optionalfeature.name: Stick and Move
entries: After you attack with a {@filter finesse weapon|items|source=|type=|property=finesse|category=|miscellaneous=}, you can spend 2 {@subclassFeature Prowess|Rogue||Duelist|MidgardHeroes|3} as a bonus action to move half your speed. This movement doesn't provoke {@action opportunity attack||opportunity attacks}.

optionalfeature.name: Tagging Strike
entries: When you hit a creature with a {@filter finesse weapon|items|source=|type=|property=finesse|category=|miscellaneous=}, you can spend 2 {@subclassFeature Prowess|Rogue||Duelist|MidgardHeroes|3} to impair your target by cutting a strap, injuring a joint, or dealing some other distracting blow. The target must make a successful Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage on the first attack roll or ability check it makes during its turn. The effect ends when the creature makes the saving throw successfully or spends an action to end it (readjusting its gear, regaining its balance, etc.)

optionalfeature.name: Wall of Flashing Steel
entries: As a reaction when you are hit by a melee weapon attack, you can spend 2 {@subclassFeature Prowess|Rogue||Duelist|MidgardHeroes|3} to attack with a {@filter finesse weapon|items|source=|type=|property=finesse|category=|miscellaneous=}. If the result of your attack roll exceeds your AC, use the attack roll in place of your AC until the start of your next turn. This can cause the triggering attack to miss.

optionalfeature.name: Witty Repartee
entries: As a reaction after a creature you can see makes an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw but before success or failure is revealed, you can spend 2 {@subclassFeature Prowess|Rogue||Duelist|MidgardHeroes|3} to encourage or mock the creature. If the creature can hear you, roll a {@dice d4}; the result is added to or subtracted from the creature's attack roll (your choice).

optionalfeature.name: Will of the Master
entries: You can cast {@spell planar binding} once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Cloud of Cogs
entries: You can cast {@spell gear barrage|MidgardHeroes} once using a warlock spell slot. You must finish a long rest before doing so again.

optionalfeature.name: Heat of the Furnace
entries: You summon up the fires from within. As long as you maintain {@status concentration}, your melee attacks do an additional {@damage 1d6} fire damage. Attackers within 5 feet of you who hit you in melee take {@damage 1d6} fire damage. This heat does not affect any of your gear.

optionalfeature.name: Voice of the Machine
entries: {@filter Spells you cast that charm or frighten creatures|spells|source=|components & miscellaneous=|conditions inflicted=charmed;frightened} overcome the immunity of constructs to being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}. Saving throws apply normally.

optionalfeature.name: Benevolence (Amnayeth)
entries: This seal allows its user to retry a failed ability check to improve another creature's attitude.

entries.name: {@b {@i Greater:}}
entries: As an action, the user can cause a creature who can hear the user to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be {@condition charmed} for 10 minutes.
This seal can't be used again on the same creature until the user finishes a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Clarity (Simil)
entries: The user adds half your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1) to its Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks.

entries.name: Greater:
entries: The user gains {@sense blindsight} with a range of 10 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Fortitude (Rikbil)
entries: When it is hit with an attack, the user can use its reaction to add 2 to its AC against that attack. It must be able to see the attacker to use this effect.

entries.name: Greater:
entries: The user gains resistance to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

optionalfeature.name: Fortune (Barrateth)
entries: The use can add 1 to an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check. The addition is made after rolling the die but before the GM reveals whether it was a success or failure.

entries.name: Greater:
entries: After making an attack roll, saving throw, or ability check, the user can roll another {@dice d20} and choose which result to use. The second roll is made after the initial roll but before the GM reveals whether that roll was a success or failure.
After using this seal, it can't be used again until the user finishes a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Glory (Vrechiel)
entries: When the user reduces an enemy to 0 hit points, the user gains {@quickref temporary hit points||3|0}{@sup {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|'24}} equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1).

entries.name: Greater:
entries: At the start of its turn, the user gains {@quickref temporary hit points||3|0}{@sup {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|'24}} equal to your wizard level.

optionalfeature.name: Humility (Ophanim)
entries: The user adds half your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1) to its Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks. It loses this bonus until the start of its next turn when it takes a hostile action (any action that could inflict intentional damage on a creature).

entries.name: Greater:
entries: If it hasn't taken a hostile action this turn, the user can use its action to present an aura of humility. A creature that wishes to attack the user must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or choose another target. If there are no other targets it wishes to attack, the creature wastes its action doing nothing.

optionalfeature.name: Judgment (Chamule)
entries: When the user hits a creature with an {@action opportunity attack}, the user can move up to half its speed, provided it ends the move closer to the creature it hit.

entries.name: Greater:
entries: As a reaction when it is hit with an attack, the user can cause the attacker to take radiant damage equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1), if the attacker is within 60 feet of the user.

optionalfeature.name: Recovery (Jelaal)
entries: The user automatically succeeds on its first death saving throw and then regains 1 hit point. Once used, this effect doesn't function again until the user finishes a short or long rest.

entries.name: Greater:
entries: When the user regains hit points, it regains an additional number of hit points equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1).

optionalfeature.name: Temperance (Iaothe)
entries: When it makes a Wisdom saving throw, the user can use its reaction to add your Intelligence modifier to the roll (minimum of 1). The bonus is added after the die is rolled but before the GM reveals whether the saving throw succeeds or fails. This ability doesn't function again until the user finishes a short or long rest.

entries.name: Greater:
entries: The user can't be {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

optionalfeature.name: Wrath (Xapanie)
entries: Once on the user's turn when it hits with a weapon attack, the attack deals 1 additional point of weapon damage.

entries.name: Greater:
entries: The user's attacks are magical, and once on the user's turn when it hits with a weapon attack, the attack deals an additional amount of radiant damage equal to half your wizard level.

optionalfeature.name: Elemental Adaptation
entries: You add {@spell plane shift} to your spellbook if you don't already have it, and you can cast it once with this mastery without expending a spell slot. You can only travel to the elemental plane associated with your elemental focus, or to the material plane. While on that elemental plane, you aren't harmed by the normal conditions of the plane, you can breathe normally, and you can use your speed to move in a manner appropriate to the plane (flying for air, burrowing for earth, and swimming for water). You regain the ability to cast {@spell plane shift} with this mastery when you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Elemental Binding
entries: You add {@spell planar binding} to your spellbook if you don't already have it, and you can cast it once with this mastery without expending a spell slot. When you cast the spell in this way, you must target an elemental creature associated with your elemental focus. If the elemental fails its saving throw, it gains temporary hit points equal to your wizard level. You regain the ability to cast {@spell planar binding} with this mastery when you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Elemental Bulwark
entries: You gain resistance to the damage type associated with your elemental focus, and you have advantage on Constitution saving throws made to maintain {@status concentration} on spells that resonate with your elemental focus.

optionalfeature.name: Elemental Command
entries: As an action, you can charm an elemental creature for as long as you maintain {@status concentration} as if {@status concentration||concentrating} on a spell, for up to 1 hour. If you or your companions damage the {@condition charmed} elemental or take any harmful action against it, the {@condition charmed} condition ends immediately. Once this ability ends for a given elemental, it is immune to being {@condition charmed} by you for 24 hours.

optionalfeature.name: Elemental Erosion
entries: After you damage a target with a spell that deals the damage type associated with your elemental focus, the target becomes vulnerable to that damage type until the end of your next turn. You can use this ability twice. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Ignite
entries: When you deal {@filter fire damage to a target with a spell of 1st level or higher|spells|source=|level=!0|components & miscellaneous=|subschool=clockwork|damage type=fire}, you can use a bonus action to cause the target to catch fire. At the start of its next turn, the creature takes half as much fire damage as it originally took from the spell, and the flames go out. This damage can be prevented if an ally within 5 feet uses an action to put out the flames. If you damage more than one target with the spell, choose just one to catch fire.

optionalfeature.name: Intensified Element
entries: Your spells that deal the damage type associated with your elemental focus ignore any resistance the target has to that damage type.

optionalfeature.name: Stony Refuge
entries: When you cast a {@filter spell of 1st level or higher|spells|source=|level=!0|components & miscellaneous=}, you can use a bonus action to create a hovering slab of stone that interposes itself between you and one creature of your choice. Until the end of your next turn, the slab provides {@quickref Cover||3||half cover} to you against attacks from the chosen creature.

optionalfeature.name: Sweeping Swell
entries: When you cast a {@filter spell of 1st level or higher|spells|source=|level=!0|components & miscellaneous=}, you can use a bonus action to create a sweeping wave of water to push a target you can see within 20 feet. The creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 5 feet in the direction of your choice.

optionalfeature.name: Wind Blast
entries: When you cast a {@filter spell of 1st level or higher|spells|source=|level=!0|components & miscellaneous=}, you can use a bonus action to create a line of wind that is 20 feet long and 5 feet wide. Each creature in the line must make a Strength saving throw or take {@damage 1d6} slashing damage and be pushed 10 feet away from you in the direction of the line.

optionalfeature.name: Algiz
entries: {@i Elk and reindeer, evading danger; the god {@deity Heimdall|Norse}}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You can make an {@item elk horn rod|MidgardHeroes} in 6 hours, and you gain a +1 bonus on initiative checks.

items.name: Rune Power (5th level):
items.entry: Living creatures that are within 10 feet of an algiz rune when it is etched on the ground or on a wall gain a +2 bonus on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks and have advantage on saving throws against sleep spells and other magical effects that cause {@condition unconscious||unconsciousness}. Both effects last for 8 hours. Scribing the rune takes 15 minutes.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (8th level):
items.entry: Marking a willing creature with the algiz rune transforms the creature into an {@creature elk} or reindeer, as per {@spell polymorph}. Drawing the rune on the creature takes 1 action and the change lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 hour.

optionalfeature.name: Ansuz
entries: {@i Gods and outsiders}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You get a +1 bonus on Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) checks made to identify the type, powers, or weaknesses of aberrations, celestials, fey, and fiends. Once per day, the ansuz rune can be sketched on an object to ward off giants and fey hostile to the Aesir. The rune must be presented boldly toward such creatures, each of which makes a Charisma saving throw. Creatures that fail the saving throw must stay at least 5 feet away from the rune and cannot touch or make melee attacks against the creature presenting it, as long as the symbol is boldly and aggressively presented (which requires an action each round). Sketching the rune takes 1 action, and the marked object remains effective for 1 hour.

items.name: Rune Power (7th level):
items.entry: Sketching ansuz on the floor or a wall allows all worshippers of the Aesir within 10 feet of it to add {@dice 1d4} to each of their attack rolls, similar to a {@spell bless} spell. Sketching the rune takes 2 actions, and it remains effective while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 minute.

items.name: Mastery Power (11th level):
items.entry: When traced on the forehead of a willing creature, ansuz sends the creature into a trance during which it receives a message from the gods, identical in effect to a {@spell divination} spell. A creature can experience only one such trance per week. Tracing the rune takes 10 minutes, and the trance lasts 1 round.

optionalfeature.name: Berkanan
entries: {@i Birch tree, love, new beginnings; the Bear Maiden}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You get a +1 bonus on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks.

items.name: Rune Powers:
entries: When traced on a living creature, this rune grants that creature advantage on a single Charisma (Persuasion) of its choosing. Tracing the rune takes 10 minutes, and it remains effective for up to 24 hours. This benefit can be granted only once per day.

When berkanan is traced on a living plant, an effect identical to {@spell spike growth} erupts in a 20-foot radius around the rune. Tracing the rune on a plant takes 1 action, and the effect lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 10 minutes.

items.name: Mastery Power (11th level):
items.entry: You can create a {@item philter of love}{@sup {@item philter of love|XDMG|'24}} once per week.

optionalfeature.name: Dagaz
entries: {@i Day and laying the dead to rest}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You always know exactly how long it will be until the next sunset or sunrise.

items.name: Rune Power (4th Level):
items.entry: Spending 10 minutes tracing dagaz onto a creature cures it of magical {@condition blinded||blindness}.

items.name: Rune Power (6th Level):
items.entry: Tracing dagaz onto an object makes it {@quickref Vision and Light|PHB|2|0|glow brightly}{@sup {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB|'24}} in a 60-foot radius and {@quickref Vision and Light|PHB|2|0|dimly}{@sup {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB|'24}} out to 120 feet. Magical darkness of 3rd level or lower is dispelled if this light overlaps it. The light is blocked normally by opaque objects or structures. Tracing the rune this way takes 1 action, and the light lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 hour.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (13th level):
items.entry: Once per week, dagaz can be sketched onto the ground or a wall to create an instantaneous effect identical to a cleric's {@classFeature Channel Divinity: Turn Undead|Cleric||2} class ability. Sketching the rune takes 1 action, and it affects undead within 30 feet that can see it at the moment of its completion.

optionalfeature.name: Ehwaz
entries: {@i Horses, freedom, the Nithing Pole}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You get a +1 bonus to Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling}) checks made to control a mount, and to Dexterity checks made to wriggle free of bonds.

items.name: Rune Power (5th Level):
items.entry: Spending 1 action to trace ehwaz onto a lock or shackles has the same effect as a {@spell knock} spell but without the loud knocking sound.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (8th level):
items.entry: When sketched on the ground, ehwaz summons a {@spell phantom steed}. Sketching the rune takes 5 minutes, and the summoned steed remains for 1 hour.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (9th level):
items.entry: You can create a {@item nithing pole|MidgardHeroes} (see the appendix) once per month. The process can only be performed during a new moon, takes 12 hours, and requires bloodletting that leaves a permanent scar in the shape of the ehwaz rune on the rune maker's left hand. Each creation of a nithing pole creates a new scar; those after the first don't need to be on the hand, but they must be on the hands or arms.

optionalfeature.name: Eiwaz
entries: {@i Yew tree, Yggdrasil}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You have proficiency on Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) checks.

items.name: Rune Power (4th Level):
items.entry: Spending 1 action to trace eiwaz onto a suitably-sized piece of wood transforms it into a {@spell shillelagh} for 1 minute.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (8th level):
items.entry: Sleeping for at least 10 minutes under a tracing of eiwaz on a blanket, a wall, or the ceiling causes the rune maker to dream of the Nine Worlds. These dreams produce answers the same as {@spell contact other plane} (including possible side effects).

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (11th level):
items.entry: When carved on the bark of a living tree, eiwaz allows the rune master to travel between that tree and another tree within 500 feet also marked with eiwaz, as if under the effect of {@spell tree stride}. Each carving takes 1 action, and the effect lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 minute after the second carving is done.

optionalfeature.name: Gebu
entries: {@i Generosity and hospitality, and gifts}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You have advantage on Intelligence checks to estimate an item's value.

items.name: Rune Powers:
items.entry: When 1 minute is spent carefully examining an item while tracing gebu on it, you learn the item's name and magical properties the same as if you'd cast an {@spell identify} spell. Alternatively, if 1 action is spent tracing gebu on a willing creature, that creature gains sanctuary as if affected by the spell. The rune maker can't invoke {@spell sanctuary} on him or her self. The sanctuary effect lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 minute.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (8th level):
items.entry: Gebu can be rapidly (1 action) scratched or sketched onto a wall, floor, or other surface of an enclosed space in a way that makes it clearly visible. For up to 1 minute, the rune maker can activate the rune with a command word, provided he or she is within 20 feet of it. All creatures within 20 feet of the rune and with a clear line of sight to it (regardless of whether they can actually see it) must make a Charisma saving throw. Those that fail are affected as if by a {@spell calm emotions} spell that lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 minute.

optionalfeature.name: Hagalaz
entries: {@i Hail and winter weather}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks made to accurately predict weather conditions for the next 24 hours in your vicinity.

items.name: Rune Power:
items.entry: Spending 1 action tracing hagalaz on a willing creature grants it {@spell protection from energy} (cold) while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 hour.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (9th level):
items.entry: When sketched on the ground under the open sky, hagalaz conjures a {@spell sleet storm} centered on a point of the rune maker's choosing within 150 feet. Sketching the rune takes 4 rounds and the storm lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 2 minutes.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (14th level):
items.entry: When sketched on the ground within the area of a magical storm caused by {@spell sleet storm}, {@spell ice storm}, {@spell control weather} (hail and blizzard only), or comparable magic, hagalaz acts as a {@spell dispel magic} spell cast with a 7th-level slot. If the storm being dispelled is from 8th or 9th level magic, the rune maker uses Charisma as his or her spellcasting ability to test the rune's effectiveness. Sketching the rune takes 2 rounds.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (14th level):
items.entry: When sketched on the ground within the area of a natural (nonmagical) storm, hagalaz has the effect of a {@spell control weather} spell. Sketching the rune takes 1 hour and the control lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 8 hours.

optionalfeature.name: Ingwaz
entries: {@i Ancestors, major deities}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You have proficiency on Intelligence ({@skill History}) checks.

items.name: Rune Power (4th level):
items.entry: When 1 action is spent tracing ingwaz on a {@item spear}, the rune gains favor from the Aesir for the first character who throws the spear over the heads of an enemy force consisting of nine or more opponents; the spear thrower adds {@damage 1d4} radiant damage to his or her weapon attacks. The bonus can be gained up to 8 hours after the rune is traced, and once triggered it lasts 1 minute.

items.name: Rune Power (5th level):
items.entry: When ingwaz is traced on the face of a willing creature, it turns the creature into a berserker, granting it the benefits of a barbarian's {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1} class feature as though the recipient were a {@class barbarian} of the rune master's level. Tracing the rune takes 2 rounds, and the effect lasts 1 minute.

items.name: Rune Power (7th level):
items.entry: You learn the {@spell calling to the Vættir|MidgardHeroes} ritual (see Rune Rituals).

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (8th level):
items.entry: When traced on the nails and flesh of a corpse, this rune acts as a {@spell speak with dead} spell. Tracing the rune takes 1 minute and the effect lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 10 minutes.

optionalfeature.name: Isaz
entries: {@i Ice, imprisonment, and paralysis; the gods Boreas and Mara}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: When you take cold damage, you take 1 point less than normal.

items.name: Rune Power (4th level):
items.entry: When 1 action is spent tracing isaz on the ground, slick ice spreads out to cover an area of 225 square feet. The iced area can have any shape the rune maker wants; if you use a grid of 5-foot squares, the ice covers 9 squares. Every square of ice must be connected to at least one other along one or more sides. Ice-filled squares are {@quickref difficult terrain||3}, and the difficulty of Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) checks in the area increases by five. The ice is nonmagical and melts normally.

items.name: Rune Power (5th level):
items.entry: When the isaz rune is traced on a weapon, the weapon sheds {@quickref Vision and Light|PHB|2|0|bright light}{@sup {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB|'24}} in a 10-foot radius and {@quickref Vision and Light|PHB|2|0|dim light}{@sup {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB|'24}} for an additional 10 feet, and it does an extra {@damage 1d4} cold damage on a hit. Tracing the rune takes 1 action and the effect lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 hour.

items.name: Rune Power (7th level):
items.entry: A shield marked with this rune glitters like frost in the morning sun. As an action, a wielder of the shield who is in direct sunlight can focus its reflection on a single enemy within 30 feet; the target must make a successful Constitution saving throw or be affected by the first stage of {@condition snow blindness|MidgardHeroes} (see the appendix). The DC for the saving throw equals 8 + your Dexterity bonus + your proficiency bonus. If the target creature is already affected by the first stage of snow blindness, a failed Constitution saving throw brings on the second stage of snow blindness. Marking the shield takes 1 action, and the rune remains effective while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 hour.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (8th level):
items.entry: You learn the {@spell create tupilak|MidgardHeroes} ritual (see Rune Rituals).

optionalfeature.name: Jera
entries: {@i Abundant harvest; the gods Freyr and Freyja}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You have proficiency on Intelligence ({@skill Nature}) checks.

items.name: Rune Power (4th level):
items.entry: When traced on the belly of a living creature, the jera rune guarantees that any sexual union involving that creature in the next 24 hours proves fruitful. If traced on a pregnant creature at least once per month during its pregnancy, the rune guarantees a successful birth and healthy baby or babies. Tracing the rune takes 1 action.

items.name: Rune Power (6th level):
items.entry: When jera is traced on a wooden table, plate, or platter, all food and drink that's spoiled, rotten, diseased, poisonous, or otherwise contaminated within 20 feet becomes pure and safe for consumption. Tracing the rune takes 1 action.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (8th level):
items.entry: When marked in the earth among living plants, the rune has the effect of a localized (100-foot radius) {@spell plant growth} spell. Sketching the rune takes 1 action.

optionalfeature.name: Kaunen
entries: {@i Flaming torch, enlightenment, and ulcers}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You gain darkvision (30 feet). If you already have darkvision, you gain proficiency on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks.

items.name: Rune Power (4th level):
items.entry: When 1 minute is spent tracing kaunen on the ground, it burns like a campfire for up to 8 hours. The fire is 2 feet in diameter and the flames are magical, causing {@damage 2d6} fire damage per round to any creature in the fire. Neither the rune nor the fire can be moved once created, but the fire can spread normally to inflammable materials.

items.name: Rune Power (5th level):
items.entry: Tracing kaunen onto a weapon causes that weapon to shed {@quickref Vision and Light|PHB|2|0|bright light}{@sup {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB|'24}} in a 10-foot radius plus {@quickref Vision and Light|PHB|2|0|dim light}{@sup {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB|'24}} for another 10 feet, and the weapon does an extra {@damage 1d6} fire damage on a hit. Tracing the rune takes 1 action and the effect lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 hour.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (8th level):
items.entry: When traced on a living creature, kaunan burns away illness and ailments the same as a {@spell lesser restoration} spell. Invoking the rune takes 1 action.

optionalfeature.name: Laukaz
entries: {@i Water, the sea; the god Njord}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You gain {@language Aquan|PHB}{@sup {@language Aquan|XPHB|'24}} as a bonus language. If you already know this language, you have advantage on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks with creatures from the elemental plane of water.

items.name: Rune Power (4th level):
items.entry: When you spend 1 action tracing laukaz on an object, it becomes more bouyant. Anyone grasping the object has advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks to swim or to stay afloat. The effect lasts 24 hours.

items.name: Rune Power (7th level):
items.entry: When you trace laukaz on a living creature's chest, it grants them {@spell water breathing}. Tracing the rune takes 1 action, and the effect lasts 24 hours.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (11th level):
items.entry: When the rune is sketched on a lakebed, riverbed, or sea bottom, laukaz allows the rune maker to control nearby water as a {@spell control water} spell. Sketching the rune takes 1 action and the effect lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 10 minutes.

optionalfeature.name: Mannaz
entries: {@i Humanoids, especially humans and dwarves}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You get a +1 bonus on saving throws against petrification and effects that alter your form such as lycanthropy, {@spell polymorph}, or {@spell flesh to stone}.

items.name: Rune Power (5th level):
items.entry: When traced on the ground, a boulder, or a wall, mannaz produces an effect identical to a {@spell calm emotions} spell on all living creatures within 20 feet of the rune. Tracing the rune takes 1 action and the effect lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 minute.

items.name: Rune Power (8th level):
items.entry: When traced on the flesh of a creature, mannaz returns a lycanthrope to its humanoid form or dispels (as {@spell dispel magic}) a polymorph effect the target is currently under. Unwilling creatures resist this with a Charisma saving throw; use your Wisdom modifier to set the DC. Tracing the rune takes 1 action.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (9th level):
items.entry: Invoking mannaz upon a plant or animal awakens it, per the {@spell awaken} spell. Invoking the rune this way takes 8 hours, and the effect is permanent.

optionalfeature.name: Naudiz
entries: {@i Necessity and need}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: Once per day, you can cast spare the dying.

items.name: Rune Power (4th level):
items.entry: By spending 1 action to trace naudiz on an item, you grant {@spell guidance} or {@spell resistance} to any creature that touches it (the creature chooses which). A number of creatures equal to your proficiency bonus can benefit from this effect. Both the rune and the effect last while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 minute.

items.name: Rune Power (5th level):
items.entry: Naudiz can be traced in the air in such a way that five creatures within 10 feet of you have their hit point maximums increased by 5, and they gain 5 hit points. Tracing the rune takes 1 action, and the effect lasts 8 hours.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (15th level):
items.entry: When naudiz is traced on an object weighing no more than 20 lb., for the next 24 hours you can call that item to you. The item teleports into your hand instantly when you spend an action calling it. The maximum range is 400 feet. If another creature is holding the object, that creature prevents it from teleporting with a successful Charisma saving throw; use your Wisdom modifier to set the DC. Tracing the rune takes just 1 action, and it remains until it's used or for 24 hours.

optionalfeature.name: Nyköping
entries: {@i The passage of time, the seasons, and the phases of the moon}

items.name: Rune Bonus:
items.entry: You have proficiency with Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks.

items.name: Rune Power (5th level):
items.entry: When the rune is sketched on the ground, nyköping allows up to six creatures of your choice to each regain hit points equal to {@dice 2d8} + your wisdom modifier. You must be able to see the creatures, and they must be within 30 feet of you. Invoking the rune this way takes 10 minutes.

items.name: Rune Power (6th level):
items.entry: When 1 action is spent tracing the rune on the ground under open sky, nyköping creates a {@spell gust of wind}. The wind lasts while you {@condition concentration||concentrate}, for up to 1 minute.

items.name: Rune Mastery Power (9th level):
